title: Mega Ampharos Battle Guide

recommendedItems.reason: Required mega stone for the Ampharos mega form

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Ampharos evolves into an [Electric]/[Dragon] bulky special breaker with 165 Special Attack, 105 Defense, and 110 Special Defense. The new [mold-breaker] ability is a critical upgrade — it ignores ability-based resistances like [volt-absorb], [lightning-rod], and [water-absorb], which means Ampharos's [Electric] STAB always lands the damage the opponent tried to avoid. 45 Speed is still the limiting factor, but the huge Special Attack and the dual bulk make Mega Ampharos one of the most effective [trick-room] breakers in the Champions format.

## Strengths
165 Special Attack is elite damage output — [thunderbolt] alone can two-shot most walls, and [dragon-pulse] rounds out the offense against [Dragon] switch-ins. [mold-breaker]'s interaction is the ability change that earns Mega Ampharos its slot: any ability-based [Electric] resistance is simply ignored. 105/110 dual bulk means Mega Ampharos survives long enough to trade multiple hits, and [volt-switch] gives it a safe exit option when a hard wall comes in.

## Weaknesses & Answers
45 Speed is the structural hole — under normal conditions Mega Ampharos moves last against almost every attacker in the format, so a [trick-room] team is effectively required to maximize its value. The [Dragon] type also adds a 4x [Ice] weakness, making [ice-beam] and other [Ice] moves one-shot threats. The pre-mega turn runs [static], which is minor but means the first hit is unpredictable.

## Recommended Moveset
[thunderbolt] is the locked [Electric] STAB for main damage. [dragon-pulse] is the second STAB and handles [Dragon] switch-ins that would otherwise resist the primary move. [focus-blast] is the coverage slot against [Rock], [Dark], and [Steel] walls — 70 percent accuracy is the main drawback. [volt-switch] fills the fourth slot to preserve tempo against hard walls. [power-gem] is an alternate coverage pick for [Flying] and [Fire] threats.

## Item · Ability · Tera
The item is always [ampharosite] — there's no alternate. The ability is locked to [mold-breaker] after evolution, and the pre-mega [static] is largely irrelevant. Tera [Electric] maxes [thunderbolt] damage for the late-game cleanup role, while Tera [Fairy] flips the [Ice] and [Dragon] weaknesses into resists and covers the defensive profile more completely.

## Teammates & Synergy
Mega Ampharos is best paired with [trick-room] setters, since 45 Speed becomes the highest on the field under [trick-room]. An [Ice] answer — a [Steel] or [Fire] partner — clears the 4x weakness hole. Physical walls partner well on the back line because Mega Ampharos's dual bulk handles special attacks but can't trade against strong physical breakers. A hazard remover preserves the bulk over a long match.

## Play Patterns
In a [trick-room] team, Mega Ampharos comes in after the room is set and starts chaining [thunderbolt] and [dragon-pulse] KOs against the now-slow opposing team. Outside [trick-room], use [volt-switch] to pivot away from unfavorable matchups, and save [focus-blast] for telegraphed [Steel] or [Dark] walls. The Tera must be held for the moment an [Ice] move is telegraphed — using it any earlier wastes the entire defensive option.
